findings.message: rename_item updates item.name but never recalculates the derived slug, so slug goes stale and diverges from name. The model contract (models.py:16-18) requires slug to stay in sync with name, and repository.update_item_name already does this correctly (sets item.slug = _make_slug(name)). This route bypasses that helper, leaving an inconsistent cross-field write. Because slug is unique+indexed, stale slugs also block legitimate future renames/collisions.
findings.suggestion: Recalculate the slug alongside the name, or delegate to the repository helper. e.g. replace the direct mutation with `from ..db.repository import update_item_name` and `item = update_item_name(session, item_id, payload.name)` (raising 404 when it returns None), or set `item.slug = _make_slug(payload.name)` before committing.
